hustles.name: Dropshipping
hustles.verdict: High risk. Low barrier to entry, but 90% of dropshippers lose money. Extreme competition, thin margins, and supplier reliability issues are the main killers.
hustles.reality: Most quit within 90 days. The 10% who succeed spend months finding reliable suppliers and building genuine expertise.
hustles.goodFit: People who love learning about e-commerce, testing products, and can handle supplier negotiations.

hustles.name: Print on Demand
hustles.verdict: Decent side hustle, but takes 6–12 months to build an audience. Crowded on Etsy, better on your own store with targeted SEO.
hustles.reality: Low startup cost, 85–95% margins, but requires consistent design output and understanding of trending niches.
hustles.goodFit: Creative people who can design, or who can hire designers. Understanding of trending niches is essential.

hustles.name: Faceless YouTube
hustles.verdict: Viable but overhyped. 6–12 months before your first dollar. YouTube's AI content crackdown is real — quality matters more than ever.
hustles.reality: $5,000–$10,000/month is achievable at scale, but requires consistent output, good SEO, and patience.
hustles.timeToMoney: 6–12 months for AdSense; faster with affiliate links
hustles.goodFit: People who enjoy researching and synthesizing information. Comfortable with AI tools for scripting and voiceover.

hustles.name: Vibe Coding (AI App Building)
hustles.verdict: Emerging opportunity. No-code + AI tools mean anyone can build apps now. The money is in finding specific niches and validating quickly.
hustles.reality: The gold rush is starting. Early movers are building micro-SaaS products in weeks, not months. But saturation is coming fast.
hustles.timeToMoney: 1–3 months to first product, 3–6 months to first sale
hustles.goodFit: People with ideas for specific niches who aren't afraid to experiment with tools like Cursor, Replit, Lovable, or bolt.new.

hustles.name: Website Development
hustles.verdict: Steady demand, but competitive. AI tools like Wix ADI and Framer are eating the simple site market. Differentiation through specialization is key.
hustles.reality: Local businesses still pay $500–$5,000 for custom sites. The key is niche specialization and delivering faster than agencies.
hustles.goodFit: People who can learn basic web tech (or use no-code tools) and are comfortable talking to small business owners.

hustles.name: AI Agents / Automation
hustles.verdict: Early mover advantage is real. Businesses are actively paying for automation setups. But the market is moving fast and technical skills matter here.
hustles.reality: Setup fees range $200–$2,000 per automation. Recurring revenue possible with retainer models. Technical but accessible with the right learning path.
hustles.goodFit: People who enjoy problem-solving, can follow tutorials, and aren't afraid of learning basic scripting or no-code automation tools.

hustles.name: UGC Creation
hustles.verdict: One of the most accessible side hustles in 2026. No followers needed. $50–$500/video. The window for easy entry is closing but still open.
hustles.reality: Start at $50–$150/piece, scale to $300–$500/piece within 6 months. Top UGC creators earn $10K+/month with retainer deals.
hustles.goodFit: People comfortable on camera (even just voice), can follow a brief, and are reliable with fast turnaround.

hustles.name: Affiliate Marketing
hustles.verdict: The 57% who earn under $10K/year quit too early or pick the wrong niche. The 43% who win do one specific thing better than everyone else.
hustles.reality: Beginners average $636/month. Top affiliates earn $8K–$15K/month. The gap is patience + niche strategy + content quality.
hustles.goodFit: People who enjoy writing or creating content, can be patient, and are comfortable with SEO basics.

hustles.name: Etsy (Printable Planners)
hustles.verdict: Still profitable, but not the 2021–2023 gold rush. Crowded but winnable with hyper-niche targeting and quality design.
hustles.reality: Under $50 to start, 85–95% margins. Top sellers do 60+ sales/month on single listings. Niche is everything.
hustles.goodFit: Creative people who can design in Canva or Illustrator, understand SEO for Etsy, and can find underserved niches.

hustles.name: Amazon FBA
hustles.verdict: High barrier to entry, high reward if done right. $4K+ startup minimum for decent chances. Review moat is brutal in 2026.
hustles.reality: $2.4B market, 40–60% margins possible, but 12–18 months to profitability. Private label is the real game, not retail arbitrage.
hustles.goodFit: People with capital to invest, tolerance for risk, and ability to source products from manufacturers.

hustles.name: Notion Templates
hustles.verdict: Market peaked in 2024. Still profitable but requires genuine niche focus and consistent output. General templates don't sell anymore.
hustles.reality: Some creators earn $80K+/year. Most earn nothing. The difference is niche targeting, design quality, and promotion.
hustles.timeToMoney: 1–3 months for first sale with right niche
hustles.goodFit: Organized people who understand productivity systems, can design well, and know how to find specific user needs.

hustles.name: Niche Newsletter
hustles.verdict: Long ramp-up but genuine path to $1K–$10K/month. The writers who win are those who pick tiny niches and provide impossible-to-find-elsewhere insights.
hustles.reality: Most newsletters take 6–12 months to reach 1,000 subscribers. At $5–$10/subscriber/month, that equals $5K–$10K/month.
hustles.goodFit: Writers who have genuine expertise or passion for a specific topic and can commit to weekly publishing.

hustles.name: Dog Supplements (Amazon / Etsy)
hustles.verdict: High margins, growing market, but $4K+ startup and brutal review moat. Only viable if you can wait 6–12 months for organic reviews.
hustles.reality: $2.4B pet supplement market growing 8% annually. Top sellers use Amazon FBA with private label. Review accumulation is the main challenge.
hustles.goodFit: People with capital, patience, and ability to navigate Amazon's TOS and supplier relationships.

hustles.name: Freelance Market Research
hustles.verdict: Counter-cyclical win. As AI makes shallow research free, deep human analysis becomes more valuable. Zero startup cost, 80–95% margins.
hustles.reality: Basic reports $100–$300, strategic deep dives $2,000–$5,000. AI actually helps freelancers deliver more value faster.
hustles.goodFit: Analytical people who enjoy research, can synthesize complex information, and communicate clearly in writing.

hustles.name: Micro-SaaS (Local Business)
hustles.verdict: Highest ceiling of any side hustle here. $5K–$50K/month possible. But requires both technical skills and sales ability.
hustles.reality: Solo founders hitting $5K–$50K/month ARR. Low overhead, recurring revenue. Success requires finding a very specific pain point and executing relentlessly.
hustles.timeToMoney: 3–6 months to first prototype, 6–12 months to first paying customer
hustles.goodFit: People who can build (or hire to build) simple software, and are comfortable selling to local businesses.

hustles.name: Surveys & Micro Tasks
hustles.verdict: Honest answer: not a real side hustle. $2–$50/month if you're lucky. Your time is worth more elsewhere.
hustles.reality: Typical earnings: $0.50–$3/hour. Only viable as supplementary income if you have literally nothing better to do.
hustles.goodFit: Honest answer: nobody. Use the time to learn a real skill instead.
